questions.question: What is the DA42-VI's VYSE (best single-engine rate of climb speed)?
questions.explanation: VYSE is 85 KIAS, marked by the blue radial line on the airspeed indicator. This is the go-to target speed after any engine failure.

questions.question: What is VFE for the LDG flap position in the DA42-VI?
questions.explanation: VFE for the LDG flap setting is 133 KIAS, which is also the upper limit of the white arc on the airspeed indicator.

questions.question: What is the normal takeoff rotation speed (VR) for the DA42-VI?
questions.explanation: The normal takeoff rotation speed (VR) for the DA42-VI is 77 KIAS. Short-field VR values differ based on weight.

questions.question: Why is VMCA with flaps UP (85 KIAS) higher than VMCA with flaps APP (71 KIAS)?

options.text: Flaps increase drag which slows the airplane, lowering VMCA
Flap extension provides aerodynamic benefits that allow directional control at a lower speed
The flap setting has no real effect; the difference is caused by weight changes
Flaps UP shifts the center of pressure aft, reducing rudder effectiveness

questions.explanation: VMCA with flaps APP (71 KIAS) is lower than with flaps UP (85 KIAS). During a go-around with an engine failure, retracting flaps too early raises your minimum controllable speed, requiring careful configuration management.

questions.question: What are the maximum landing gear speeds (VLO and VLE) for the DA42-VI?

options.text: 133 KIAS for extension, 188 KIAS for retraction
119 KIAS for extension, 152 KIAS with gear extended

questions.explanation: The DA42-VI keeps it simple — VLO for retraction, VLO for extension, and VLE (max speed with gear extended) are all 188 KIAS.
